phrases: how do i get the next chapter
where do i get the next chapter

top_posts.caption:
You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

You block her, and a week later you are back on the page. You journal it out, and the loop starts again by morning. You pray against it, and it returns by Thursday. Not because you are weak. Because you are fighting the output and leaving the source running.

The source is idolatry.

Somewhere underneath the obsession, you gave her a role that only God can fill. Source of worth. Proof of value. Evidence that you are enough. And until that displacement is addressed, the thoughts will keep generating because the root is still being fed.

Kill the idolatry and the thoughts starve.

Not suppress them. Not white-knuckle through them. Starve them. Remove the thing feeding them at the root, and the symptom stops having anything to run on.

This is not a thought management problem. It is a worship problem. And worship problems have worship solutions.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes. 

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step. A free video I made explaining

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step. A free video I made explaining
why you can’t stop thinking about her.

Most men are trying to escape the very thing God is using to build them.

They treat pain like a problem to be solved.

They want it gone. They pray it away. They distract it away. And when it is still there on Tuesday morning, they conclude that something is broken, either in them or in God’s plan.

Here is what no one told you. The pain is not the problem. The pain is the equipment.

Think about a gym. You do not walk in hoping the weights feel lighter this week. You show up because resistance is the only thing that builds anything. Pull the weight. Feel the burn. Walk out stronger. That is not a punishment. That is the mechanism.

That is how strength works. And you think God operates differently?

When God allows heartbreak, loss, or a season that feels like silence, He is not withholding comfort from you. He is offering you a bar to press.

You have a choice every single morning. Go to the gym or go to the bar. Lift the weight of the cross or buy yourself another round of comfort and stay exactly the same.

“Skinny fat” is real. A man who avoids hard training but does not visibly look broken. Functioning. Fine. But soft underneath.

That is what spiritual avoidance produces. A man who looks fine, until life hits him.

The men who come out of painful seasons with clarity, with character, with settled confidence did not get lucky. They went to the gym. They let the pain do what pain is designed to do.

You cannot get spiritually strong on a comfortable plan.

So if your chest is heavy right now, good. That means there is weight on the bar.

“What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?” Romans 8:31

You are not lifting alone.

If you are done numbing and ready to build, type I’ll lift.

And follow this page if you want your feed to strengthen your faith, not soften it.

The men who come out of painful seasons with clarity, with character, with settled confidence did not get lucky. They went to the gym. They let the pain do what pain is designed to do.

You cannot get spiritually strong on a comfortable plan.

So if your chest is heavy right now, good. That means there is weight on the bar.

“What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?” Romans 8:31

You are not lifting alone.

If you are done numbing and ready to build, type I’ll lift.

And follow this page if you want your feed to strengthen your faith, not soften it.

Most men are trying to escape the very thing God is using to build them.

They treat pain like a problem to be solved.

They want it gone. They pray it away. They distract it away. And when it is still there on Tuesday morning, they conclude that something is broken, either in them or in God’s plan.

Here is what no one told you. The pain is not the problem. The pain is the equipment.

Think about a gym. You do not walk in hoping the weights feel lighter this week. You show up because resistance is the only thing that builds anything. Pull the weight. Feel the burn. Walk out stronger. That is not a punishment. That is the mechanism.

That is how strength works. And you think God operates differently?

When God allows heartbreak, loss, or a season that feels like silence, He is not withholding comfort from you. He is offering you a bar to press.

You have a choice every single morning. Go to the gym or go to the bar. Lift the weight of the cross or buy yourself another round of comfort and stay exactly the same.

“Skinny fat” is real. A man who avoids hard training but does not visibly look broken. Functioning. Fine. But soft underneath.

That is what spiritual avoidance produces. A man who looks fine, until life hits him.

You cannot get spiritually strong on a comfortable plan.

So if your chest is heavy right now, good. That means there is weight on the bar.

And follow this page if you want your feed to strengthen your faith, not soften it.

The painful truth that follows will hurt. But it changed my life and if you let it, it will change yours too.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:

“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness,
and all these things will be added to you.”
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful and hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step. A free video I made explaining
why you can’t stop thinking about her.
